split_addresses builds addresses char-by-char due to quote/comment parsing. Returning borrowed slices would require tracking byte offsets through the state machine — a rewrite with no measurable benefit since email headers are small. The allocation is proportional to input size.

Fixed in crate-sieve-form-a3r.14 commit — advance() now has a doc comment with # Panics section.
lexer/form modules now pub(crate). Form, Stmt, Script re-exported at crate root. CompiledScript::script() added for form tree inspection.

Won't fix. Callers get &[Stmt] from script() — read-only. Construction is internal. Newtypes add Deref/Debug/Clone boilerplate for no current benefit. v0.1.0 can take a semver break if spans are added later.

Keep Vec<SieveAction>. RFC 5228 §2.10.2 requires multiple simultaneous actions (e.g. fileinto + keep). The type is correct for the spec; the single-action return is a documented implementation limitation, not a contract. Callers who iterate are writing correct code. Changing to single now means changing back when multi-action lands.
evaluate() now returns EvalResult { actions, warnings }. Regex execution errors produce EvalWarning::RegexExecutionError. Action behavior unchanged — failed matches still return false.

Won't fix. Size limits are caller policy, not library concern. Callers already have natural size boundaries (SMTP limits, DB columns, network buffers). Docs already state caller responsibility. If CompileOptions is added for other reasons later, max_size can be included then.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Acknowledged as good design — no action needed.
Both scans are now necessary — .6 fix requires finding both separators to take the minimum. A single-pass approach would add complexity for no measurable gain on email-sized headers.

lexer and form modules are now pub(crate) per .39 fix. Re-exported types (Form, Stmt, Script) have doc comments. Rustdoc examples require a public compile() → script() pipeline which is already demonstrated in the crate-level docs and tests. Adding redundant examples to each type adds maintenance burden.

Won't fix. The eq_ignore_ascii_case is redundant but correct and defensive — it ensures correctness regardless of whether extract_headers lowercases. Changing to == with lowercased keys trades one redundancy for another with more risk.

Won't fix. The encode_utf8 + stack buffer is zero-alloc, correct, and idiomatic for passing a single char to a &str-taking function. Alternatives (to_string) allocate. The pattern is verbose but efficient.

Won't fix. The separation between validation (fallible, returns Result) and caching (infallible) is intentional. Merging them mixes concerns. The coupling is documented with a comment. The risk of drift is low given both use for_each_clause.
Won't fix. O(H*N*K) is ~400 iterations for typical email. Pre-filtering headers would add a temporary allocation and complexity for no measurable gain. Email headers are small by design (RFC 5322).

Won't fix. str_contains with case-insensitive sliding window is O(n*m) but n and m are header/key lengths — typically tens of bytes. stdlib SIMD search after lowercasing would allocate the lowered strings. The current approach avoids allocation. Not a hot path bottleneck.
